title: campanula rotundifolia subsp. pennina
intro: Campanula rotundifolia subsp. pennina, also known as the bluebell of Scotland, is a subspecies of the Campanula rotundifolia plant, a flowering perennial native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The medicinal properties of Campanula rotundifolia subsp. pennina include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds, which contribute to its traditional use in treating wounds, fever, and various infections. The health benefits of this plant are attributed to its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory health, and support the immune system. Campanula rotundifolia subsp. pennina can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in herbal remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
